description_long: This is a stone bust sculpture of a man, mounted on a rectangular pedestal. The bust depicts the head and upper torso of a male figure with short, wavy hair, wearing a suit jacket, shirt, and bow tie. The facial expression is solemn, with the head slightly tilted downward and to the side. The pedestal is inscribed with the name 'JOSEF MERHAUT' and the word 'SPISOVATEL' above it, indicating the subject is likely a writer. The surface of the sculpture shows signs of weathering, with visible stains and some roughness, especially on the back and sides. The overall color is a mottled gray and white, typical of aged stone or concrete.
description: A weathered stone bust of a man in formal attire, mounted on a rectangular pedestal inscribed with 'JOSEF MERHAUT'. The sculpture features a solemn expression and a textured, aged surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, suit, and the inscribed pedestal with the name 'JOSEF MERHAUT'.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the head and the smooth, unadorned side of the pedestal.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the head, shoulders, and the plain back of the pedestal with visible weathering.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face and the right side of the pedestal, with some surface cracks visible.
attribute_subject_matter: bust of Josef Merhaut, a male writer
attribute_location_context: No clear environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
